title: File-as-scheduler: pending_prompts/ filename-encoded schedule pattern
desc: Zero-memory scheduler: filename encodes trigger time + repeat + countdown. No DB, no daemon, no in-memory state. Survives process restart/reinstall automatically. The file IS the schedule.
steps: INSIGHT: Store deferred prompts as files in {cwd}/.wkappbot/pending_prompts/. Filename encodes everything -- no JSON parse needed for scheduling decisions.
Ns][,cnt=N].json. triggerUtc=yyyyMMddHHmmss (lexicographic sort = chronological sort). NOW = session-miss retry (no time gate).
EXAMPLES: prompt_NOW_abc12345.json (session-miss, deliver asap), prompt_20260429090000_def,rep=24h.json (daily forever), prompt_20260429090000_ghi,rep=24h,cnt=5.json (daily 5 times then done).
TIME GATE: string.Compare(trigger, nowTag) <= 0 -- no JSON parse, no DateTime.Parse. NOW always passes. Sorted files give chronological delivery order.
LIFECYCLE: rename on delivery → update trigger to next time, decrement cnt. cnt=0 → delete. rep without cnt → rename forever. File born → evolves → dies. Eye holds ZERO state in memory.
ATOMIC CLAIM (race-free): File.Move(f, f+'.processing', overwrite:false). Only one thread wins. Loser skips. Error → rename back to .json for next drain retry.

DRAIN TRIGGERS: (1) OnCommandArrived: any wkappbot cmd from that CWD fires immediately. (2) Eye startup AuditOnStartup. (3) Successful prompt delivery auto-drains siblings. (4) deliver_after: skip until time.
SESSION-MISS: no target session → re-append with exponential backoff (30s→300s, infinite retries). No Slack escalation. No retry limit. File waits silently.
DURABILITY: wkappbot uninstalled → files survive. Reinstall → AuditOnStartup finds files → delivers. The OS is the persistence layer.
BEAUTY: cnt=5 recurring = 5 files created once, each self-destructs after delivery. OR one file with cnt=5 that renames itself 5 times then deletes. Same result, different philosophy.
author: claude
